title: To authorize the waiver of costs of activities relating to evacuation of United States citizens when their lives are endangered by war or acts of terrorism.
plain_summary: This bill lets the Secretary of State cancel the money that American citizens usually have to pay back when the government helps them escape from dangerous places. Right now, when Americans need to be rescued from other countries because of wars or terrorist attacks, they have to pay the government back for the cost of getting them out. This new law would make those emergency rescues free for Americans whose lives are in danger. The government would not require people to pay back the money it costs to evacuate them from these scary situations.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Waiver of Costs of Activities Relating to Evacuation of United States Citizens When Their Lives Are Endangered by War or Acts of Terrorism
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the State Department Basic Authorities Act of nineteen fifty-six. It adds a new rule that allows the Secretary of State to cancel the costs that American citizens would normally have to pay when the government helps them escape from dangerous places. This would apply when Americans need to be evacuated because their lives are in danger from war or terrorist attacks. The rule specifically applies to something called the repatriations loan program, which is a system where the government normally lends money to Americans who need help getting home from other countries and expects them to pay it back later.
